entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2025 - What is Happening in Week 3
entry.content: @NeelimaEmmani it looks like you might be missing some roles. Ensure to have the right roles assigned. Which tutorial are you trying to complete? also, in which environment is your BTP trial account?

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2025 - What is Happening in Week 3
entry.content: @NeelimaEmmani in which region is your subaccount? Ideally you would be using the Singapore region for Integration Suite. That said, if you have the right roles and you are still getting unauthorized... I would try using a different browser and incognito mode.

entry.title: Re: Why Devtoberfest keeps me coming back
entry.content: I'm very lucky to be one of the organisers for Devtoberfest..... For me it is on-going activity all year long and I enjoy it very much. Capturing ideas for next year and keeping an eye out for cool content that we can include as part of Devtoberfest. Funny enough, I started today capturing ideas for Devtoberfest 2026. Glad to know that you enjoy Devtoberfest very much 🙂

entry.title: Re: Activate Joule Studio in 5 Simple Steps
entry.content: You can also check out the content for the AI164 - Getting started with Joule: Provisioning and Contextualization hands-on workshop where these details are also explained - https://github.com/SAP-samples/teched2025-AI164

entry.title: 🇫🇷 Real-time data for AI agents: Grounding with event-driven architecture (Paris, France)
entry.content:
This CodeJam introduces attendees to the power of event-driven integration pattern to deliver real-time grounding data, equipping AI system's with the latest business context to make informed and accurate decisions. We’ll explore how to apply real-time vector grounding by connecting structured data to an LLM using a vector database using SAP Integration Suite and SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh. You’ll see how updates to the data can instantly influence the model’s responses, keeping them current and contextually accurate.

By the end of this session, you’ll achieve a scenario like the one on the screenshot below and understand the core principles behind real-time vector grounding, how to design an embedding strategy for structured entities, and how to integrate this into a sample AI workflow.

Currently, much of the session is based on the TechEd hands-on tutorials for IN162.

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser such as Google Chrome or Firefox installed on your laptop.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have, to have the instructions on a separate screen.

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Thursday, February 12, 2026 from 09:30 - 14:00 local time in Paris, France (CET). The language of the event and the content will be English.

Event Address: BearingPoint Offices at 1 Passerelle des Reflets, 92400 Courbevoie

  • The event is kindly being hosted by BearingPoint – THANK YOU!
  • The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates Antonio Maradiaga and Daniel Wroblewski

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 30 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. About 2 weeks before the event, you will be asked to confirm your attendance and will receive further instructions.

entry.title: 🇨🇭 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (Risch-Rotkreuz, Switzerland)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need to have any background in the topic, but a lot of curiosity!

In this CodeJam you will learn how to use Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement a retrieval augmented generation (RAG) use case to improve the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduce hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core and query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Furthermore, you will learn about the most important genAI concepts and create and use embeddings to improve your RAG response and build your own chatbot with memory.

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser such as Google Chrome or Firefox installed on your laptop.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have, to have the instructions on a separate screen.

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Tuesday, March 16, 2026 from 09:30 - 15:00 local time in Risch-Rotkeuz, Switzerland (CET). The language of the event and the content will be English.

Event Address: GAMBIT Consulting AG, Suurstoffi 22, 6343 Risch-Rotkreuz, Switzerland (8th floor)

  • The event is kindly being hosted by GAMBIT – THANK YOU!
  • The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates @ajmaradiaga and @Dan_Wroblewski.

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 22 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. About 2 weeks before the event, you will be asked to confirm your attendance and will receive further instructions.

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Madrid 2026 - Apr 17, 2026
entry.content:
¡Hola Madrid!

We are very excited to announce that the SAP Inside Track Madrid is back in 2026. It will take place next April 17, 2026 @ the EPAM Neoris AI Hub in Madrid, 🇪🇸. The space for the event is limited and we can only host 70 attendees. Make sure to RSVP fast!

An SAP Inside Track (SIT) is a local grassroots community event where SAP Community members come together to share knowledge about SAP related topics. The event is driven/organized by the community, everyone who is interested may participate. Everyone can benefit or contribute: act as presenter, take part in discussions, exchange information, realize networking or enjoy the social get-togethers in the evening.

You can check out all the details about the event in https://sitmadrid.com. Also, if you are interested in speaking 🗣️ at the event? Let us know by submitting your proposal here.

Come to learn and share your knowledge with your peers as well as make new friends in an informal atmosphere. SAP Inside Track events offer SAP Community provided sessions from SAP Experts and passionate SAP Community members.

We will also host an SAP CodeJam the day before (Apr 16, 2026) - 🇪🇸 TBA (Madrid, Spain). You can register for the event here - TBA.

Special thanks to our event sponsors: EPAM Neoris, SAP, SAP Press, and Sulzer.

The space for the event is limited and we can only host 70 attendees. First come first serve. We will manage a waitlist so even if we've reached 70 RSVPs please still register as some might not be able to make it to the event.

See you in April 2026!

entry.title: 🇧🇷 Joule Studio: Create Joule Skills and Agents Without Coding (São Paulo, Brazil)
entry.content:
This is the all-new SAP CodeJam for Joule Studio, the new tool in SAP Build that lets you create extensions to Joule so users can use SAP's digital assistant in their custom workflows.

The CodeJam is intended to introduce you to the Joule Studio tool, so you can create your own:

  • Skills, which let you create predefined execution flows, triggered by a user's prompt inside the Joule chatbot
  • Agents, which lets you define complex scenarios that the agent can execute by iteratively reasoning and interacting with their environment to achieve a goal, using a selected LLM, and triggered by a user's prompt inside the Joule chatbot

The session will include a short, simple introduction to the SAP AI tools and some basic concepts needed for working with Joule Studio – such as AI Core, document grounding, embeddings, and what after all is an LLM.

Currently, the event is based on these tutorials: Joule Studio CodeJam

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser of either Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge. Other browsers are not supported.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have, to have the instructions on a separate screen.

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Tuesday, March 3, 2026 from 09:00 - 14:00 local time in São Paulo, Brazil. The language of the event and the content will be English, with some support in Portuguese.

Event Address: SAP Brasil - Avenida das Nações Unidas - Vila Gertrudes, São Paulo - State of São Paulo, Brazil

  • The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates @KevinR and @ajmaradiaga.

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 30 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. About 2 weeks before the event, you will be asked to confirm your attendance and will receive further instructions.

entry.title: 🇧🇷 Build AI services using the CAP and Generative AI Hub (Campinas, Brazil)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, SAP AI Core and Generative AI Hub. It is beneficial if you have some basic knowledge about JavaScript as we're using the SAP Cloud SDK for AI with the JavaScript variant.

This CodeJam introduces attendees to aspects of exposing generative artificial intelligence (genAI) capabilities to execute tasks using a given business context.

During this event, you'll become acquainted with the theory behind genAI, SAP AI Core, SAP AI Launchpad, and learn how to use the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model to expose AI capabilities through your own OData service.

While working through the exercises, you'll become comfortable creating CDS-based service definitions and integrating them into the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and SAP AI Core Foundation Models.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Monday, March 2, 2026 in Campinas, Brazil 🇧🇷 from 09:00 - 15:00. The event is kindly being hosted by Bosch. The day of the event you will need to access the premises via Bosch Portaria 4, please be there way ahead of time, as it can take some time to go through security and get to where the event will be within the premises (No dia do evento você precisará acessar as instalações pela Bosch Portaria 4, por favor chegue com bastante antecedência, pois pode levar algum tempo para passar pela segurança e chegar até onde o evento será realizado dentro das instalações).

The content and event will be held in English with some support in Portuguese.

The instructor is the Developer Advocate @KRiedelsheimer, @ajmaradiaga  .

Prerequisites: Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Google Chrome.

Please note that seating is limited - we can only accommodate 35 attendees. Register here in the Community Event using the RSVP functionality.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: 🇧🇷 SAP Build: Create Event-Based Processes (São Paulo, Brazil)
entry.content:
This SAP Build low-code/no-code SAP CodeJam focuses on giving you hands-on experience for building event-triggered processes with SAP Build Process Automation, and how to work with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh to send those events to your processes.

You will build an event-based scenario that lets users create S/4HANA business partners, setting off a process where:

  1. An employee approves the creation of a site access badge.
  2. A second employee, determined based on the details of the business partner, may also need to approve the badge.
  3. The process calls an external, 3rd-party badge system and waits to be called back.
  4. A user creates the badge and the badge system calls back the process instance.
  5. A notification with the badge ID is sent.

The scenario is built in 2 halves:

  • SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh: You will get to know all about events, CloudEvents, events in the SAP landscape, the process for publishing and subscribing to events, and your way around SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh. Then you will create a REST Delivery Point in advanced event mesh to forward events to your SAP Build on your SAP BTP trial account.
  • SAP Build Process Automation: You will create a process from scratch to handle the issuance of site access badges. You will learn the basics of SAP Build Process Automation (projects, triggers, conditions, creating instances, monitoring, and the Inbox), and also use some of the more recent or advanced features (event triggers, form events, waiting for a API callback).

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser such as Google Chrome or Firefox installed on your laptop.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have.
  • Complete these prerequisites BEFORE you come: 
    • Introduction to the CodeJam
    • Prerequisites

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Wednesday, March 4, 2026 from 09:00 - 14:00 local time in São Paulo, Brazil (CET). The language of the event and the content will be English with some support in Portuguese.

Event Address: Exed Consulting (SAP AppHaus) R. James Joule, 65, 5º andar, Cidade Monções, São Paulo - SP, 04576-080

  • The event is kindly being hosted by Exed Consulting – THANK YOU!
  • The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates @KevinR and @ajmaradiaga .

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 30 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. About 2 weeks before the event, you will be asked to confirm your attendance.

entry.title: 🇧🇷 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (Rio de Janeiro, Brazil)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on Getting Started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need any background in the topic, but you should have a lot of curiosity!

In this CodeJam, you will learn how to use the Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). This approach improves the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduces hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core, and how to query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Additionally, you will gain insights into key generative AI concepts and learn how to enhance RAG responses.

This is a free in-person event and is planned for Thursday, March 5, 2026 in Rio de Janeiro from 09:00 to 15:00. The language of the event and the content will be in English with some support in Portuguese.

The event is kindly hosted by INFNET Institute. Thank you!

The instructors are Developer Advocates @KevinR and @ajmaradiaga  

Location: INFNET Institute - Rua São José - Centro, Rio de Janeiro - State of Rio de Janeiro, Brazil

Please note that spaces are limited; we can only accommodate 30 attendees. If you wish to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. A few weeks before the event, we will ask you to confirm your attendance.

Prerequisites: Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Microsoft Edge, Google Chrome etc.

Visit the SAP CodeJam group for more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees.

entry.title: SAP CodeJam Roadshow 2026 - Brazil edition 🇧🇷 (Kicking off in March and continuing in April)
entry.content:
Oi SAP Developers!

I've got some exciting news to share with you. The SAP CodeJam roadshow is coming back to Latin America! This time,  we will do a special edition in Brazil.  We will have many stops on the roadshow starting the 1st week of March, doing CodeJams as part of SAP Inside Tracks, organised by local community members, and then continuing  in April. Also, we'll organise a couple of meet-ups as we've done in previous year. :-).

Keep an eye on this blog post as more events will be announced as part of the roadshow shortly

SAP CodeJam events and meetups part of the roadshow (Register Now!)

Below is a listing of the SAP CodeJam events and quedadas (meetups) on the roadshow schedule:

Dates

CodeJam topic

Location

Registration

2 Mar

Build AI services using the CAP and Generative AI Hub

Campinas, Brazil 🇧🇷

Link

3 Mar

Joule Studio: Create Joule Skills and Agents Without Coding

São Paulo, Brazil 🇧🇷

🇧🇷

Link

3 Mar

São Paulo meetup

São Paulo, Brazil 🇧🇷

Link

4 Mar

SAP Build: Create Event-Based Processes

São Paulo, Brazil 🇧🇷

Link

5 Mar

Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core

Rio de Janeiro, Brazil 🇧🇷

Link

13 Mar

Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core

Joinville, Brazil 🇧🇷

Link

14 Mar

SAP Inside Track - Joinville

Joinville, Brazil 🇧🇷

Link

20 Mar

Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core

Belo Horizonte, Brazil 🇧🇷

Link

21 Mar

SAP Inside Track - Belo Horizonte

Belo Horizonte, Brazil 🇧🇷

Link

6 Apr

Combine SAP CAP with SAP HANA Cloud to Create Full-Stack App

São Paulo, Brazil 🇧🇷🇧🇷

Link

7 Apr

ABAP AI: Capabilities in SAP Joule for Developers CodeJam

São Paulo, Brazil 🇧🇷🇧🇷

Link

8 Apr

Coming soon....

Londrina, Brazil 🇧🇷

 

10 Apr

Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core

Curitiba, Brazil 🇧🇷🇧🇷

Link

13 April - 17 April

TBA

More locations to be announced soon!

 

Make sure to register to secure your place. It is likely that the places available will go fast.

What if the CodeJam topics do not cover the SAP technology that you focus on? We've organised additional local meetups just to get together and have a chat. See you all somewhere in Brazil on the roadshow!

entry.title: Re: 🇨🇭 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (Risch-Rotkreuz, Switzerland)
entry.content: @Stephan_Heinberg , I will be able to join the 16th of March, as the 17th I have a flight back home 🙂

entry.title: Meet up - São Paulo 2026-03-03 #BrazilRoadshow2026
entry.content:
The local SAP Community in São Paulo has a chance to meet after the first of two SAP CodeJams in São Paulo, kicking off the SAP CodeJam Roadshow 2026 - Brazil edition 🇧🇷!

The meet up in São Paulo will happen on March 3rd, 2026 at 18:00 BRT for a few hours, so join us and be a part of our Group!  You don't have to be an attendee of our SAP CodeJam events, just someone active or curious about the SAP Community and our focus on #developers

Let’s talk about what's new in the SAP world, about interesting SAP projects in the region, and what's new with ourselves.

Please let us know if you will participate in the meet up by clicking "I will attend", we have a table reservation for 12 at the Tatu Bola Bar.  FYI, everyone is responsible for their own to buy a food/drink item if they wish, we are just happy for the chance to connect in person and have some relaxing conversation!

Click here to find more information about the  SAP CodeJam Roadshow 2026 - Brazil edition 🇧🇷.

and we also invite you join the São Paulo Group if you are not yet the member:  São Paulo group

entry.title: Design-First Event-Driven Architecture: Mastering Multi-Account AEM Management with SAP Event Portal
entry.content:
Join us for an insightful collaboration between Solace and SAP as we explore how to build robust, scalable event-driven architectures using a design-first approach with SAP Event Portal. This session will demonstrate how Event Portal empowers organizations to design, visualize, and govern their event-driven systems while seamlessly administering Advanced Event Mesh (AEM) brokers across multiple SAP BTP sub-accounts.

What You'll Learn

  • Design-First EDA Principles: Discover how a design-first approach accelerates development, improves collaboration, and ensures consistency across your event-driven architecture

  • Event Portal Capabilities: Learn how to leverage Event Portal to design event schemas, define application domains, and create a comprehensive catalog of your event-driven assets

  • Multi-Account AEM Management: Master the administration and lifecycle management of AEM brokers spanning multiple SAP BTP sub-accounts from a centralized control plane

  • Governance and Best Practices: Understand how to implement governance policies, maintain version control, and enforce standards across your distributed event mesh

entry.title: 🇧🇷 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (Joinville, Brazil)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on Getting Started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need any background in the topic, but you should have a lot of curiosity!

In this CodeJam, you will learn how to use the Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). This approach improves the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduces hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core, and how to query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Additionally, you will gain insights into key generative AI concepts and learn how to enhance RAG responses.

This is a free in-person event and is planned for Friday, March 13, 2026 in Joinville from 09:00 to 15:00. The language of the event is in Portuguese and the content is in English.

The event is part of the SIT Joinville that will take place the following day.

The instructors is @jessicademarchi!

Location: Ágora Tech Park, Rua Dona Francisca, 8300 - Distrito Industrial, Joinville

Please note that spaces are limited; we can only accommodate 30 attendees. If you wish to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. A few weeks before the event, we will ask you to confirm your attendance.

Prerequisites: Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Microsoft Edge, Google Chrome etc.

Visit the SAP CodeJam group for more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees.

entry.title: 🇧🇷 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (Belo Horizonte, Brazil)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on Getting Started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need any background in the topic, but you should have a lot of curiosity!

In this CodeJam, you will learn how to use the Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). This approach improves the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduces hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core, and how to query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Additionally, you will gain insights into key generative AI concepts and learn how to enhance RAG responses.

This is a free in-person event and is planned for Friday, March 20, 2026 in Belo Horizonte from 09:00 to 15:00. The language of the event is in Portuguese and the content is in English.

The event is part of the SIT Belo Horizonte that will take place the following day.

The instructors are @celofiorito and @EvertonMazzer !

Location: Localiza Labs, R. Gurupá, 33 - Cachoeirinha, Belo Horizonte - MG, 31150-180, Brasil

Please note that spaces are limited; we can only accommodate 40 attendees. If you wish to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. A few weeks before the event, we will ask you to confirm your attendance.

Prerequisites: Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Microsoft Edge, Google Chrome etc.

Visit the SAP CodeJam group for more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees.

entry.title: 🇧🇷 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (Curitiba, Brazil)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on Getting Started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need any background in the topic, but you should have a lot of curiosity!

In this CodeJam, you will learn how to use the Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). This approach improves the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduces hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core, and how to query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Additionally, you will gain insights into key generative AI concepts and learn how to enhance RAG responses.

This is a free in-person event and is planned for Friday, April 10, 2026 in Curitiba from 09:00 to 15:00. The language and content of the event is in English with some support in Portuguese.

Thanks to CHpro Solutions for sponsoring the event.

The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates @thomas_jung and @RichHeilman!

Location: R. Imac. Conceição, 1155 - Prado Velho, Curitiba - PR, 80215-901

Please note that spaces are limited; we can only accommodate 26 attendees. If you wish to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. A few weeks before the event, we will ask you to confirm your attendance.

Prerequisites: Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Microsoft Edge, Google Chrome etc.

Visit the SAP CodeJam group for more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees.

entry.title: 🇧🇷 Combine SAP CAP with SAP HANA Cloud to Create Full-Stack App (São Paulo, Brazil)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event will be about Combining the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model with SAP HANA Cloud to Create Full-Stack Applications.

Join us for this day event of developer networking and hands-on learning at the SAP offices in São Paulo, Brazil.

In this CodeJam we will learn how to use an instance of SAP HANA Cloud and develop a multi-target application using SAP Business Application Studio using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP). Using CAP, you will implement a service layer as well as a SAP Fiori UI. We will get familiar with SAP HANA native artifacts, such as calculation views to enhance functionality of the application we are building.

You can make yourself familiar with the prerequisites and the learning content through the CodeJam content repository on GitHub - https://github.com/SAP-samples/cap-hana-exercises-codejam.

This free, hands-on, in-person event (not virtual) will take place on Monday, April 6th 2026, in São Paulo, Brazil at the SAP Brasil offices.

The instructors for this event are the Developer Advocates @thomas_jung and @RichHeilman .

There are essential prerequisites that every attendee must complete before arriving at the event location. You can find the prerequisites here: https://github.com/SAP-samples/cap-hana-exercises-codejam/blob/main/prerequisites.md

Please note that spaces are limited; we can only accommodate 30 attendees. If you wish to attend, please indicate that using the "Yes" button in the Will you be attending? box on this page. If you decide not to come, please make sure to unregister using the "No" button in the Will you be attending? box to open up your seat for others.

entry.title: 🇧🇷 ABAP AI: Capabilities in SAP Joule for Developers CodeJam (São Paulo, Brazil)
entry.content:
In this CodeJam session, you will learn how to use ABAP capabilities in SAP Joule for Developers, including predictive code completion, unit test generation for ABAP CDS and ABAP code, and code explanation to speed up your development with ABAP Cloud.

Join us for this day event of developer networking and hands-on learning at the SAP offices in São Paulo, Brazil.

You can make yourself familiar with the prerequisites and the learning content through the CodeJam content repository on GitHub - TBD.

This free, hands-on, in-person event (not virtual) will take place on Tuesday, April 7th 2026, in São Paulo, Brazil at the SAP Brasil offices.

The instructors for this event are the Developer Advocates @thomas_jung and @RichHeilman .

There are essential prerequisites that every attendee must complete before arriving at the event location. You can find the prerequisites here: TBD

Please note that spaces are limited; we can only accommodate 30 attendees. If you wish to attend, please indicate that using the "Yes" button in the Will you be attending? box on this page. If you decide not to come, please make sure to unregister using the "No" button in the Will you be attending? box to open up your seat for others.
